{
  "judge_id": "your_judge_id",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.3": 3, "R1.4": 3, "R1.7": 3, "RX.1": 3, "RX.3": 2, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 1},
  "rationale": {"R1.3": "The review flags a potential issue with the JPICE baseline comparison, suggesting the reviewer did not fully verify the claim against evidence.", "R1.4": "The review identifies a prior work (JPICE) and articulates a delta in terms of computational efficiency and temperature-awareness.", "R1.7": "The review mentions missing citations and suggests verifying the JPICE citation, indicating some level of engagement with citations.", "RX.1": "The review expresses uncertainty about the JPICE baseline and its performance, suggesting an awareness of potential limitations and uncertainties.", "RX.3": "The review highlights a potential issue with the dataset comparison, implying that the scope of the analysis might not be fully aligned with the requested set.", "RX.4": "The review does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the draft.", "RX.5": "The review suggests rewriting parts of the draft and mentions tracing changes, indicating some level of engagement with provenance and traceability."},
  "confidence": {"R1.3": 0.7, "R1.4": 0.8, "R1.7": 0.6, "RX.1": 0.6, "RX.3": 0.5, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.7},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The review flags a potential issue with the JPICE baseline comparison, suggesting the reviewer did not fully verify the claim against evidence.
rationale.R1.4: The review identifies a prior work (JPICE) and articulates a delta in terms of computational efficiency and temperature-awareness.
rationale.R1.7: The review mentions missing citations and suggests verifying the JPICE citation, indicating some level of engagement with citations.
rationale.RX.1: The review expresses uncertainty about the JPICE baseline and its performance, suggesting an awareness of potential limitations and uncertainties.
rationale.RX.3: The review highlights a potential issue with the dataset comparison, implying that the scope of the analysis might not be fully aligned with the requested set.
rationale.RX.4: The review does not identify any harmful content or regressions in the draft.
rationale.RX.5: The review suggests rewriting parts of the draft and mentions tracing changes, indicating some level of engagement with provenance and traceability.
